title: desmodium seleri
intro: Desmodium seleri, also known as Seler's tick trefoil or Seler's sensitive plant, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, leading to several health benefits such as reduced pain and inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being. Desmodium seleri can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the treatment of various health conditions.
